definition: pi-glossary is a Pi extension that lazy-loads glossary definitions into the agent context when user prompts mention matching terms. It reads global glossary entries from ~/.pi/agent/glossary.json and project-specific entries from .pi/glossary.json. See [[pi-glossary schema]] when adding or modifying glossary entries.

Entry schema for glossary.json: each entry is an object with required `term` (non-empty string canonical handle) and `definition` (non-empty string injected when matched). Optional fields are `aliases` (array of strings), `pattern` (custom regex trigger that overrides term and aliases matching), `flags` (regex flags, defaults to `iu`), `enabled` (boolean; set false to disable), and `source` (provenance label included with injected context). JSON Schema-style shape: `{ "type": "object", "required": ["term", "definition"], "properties": { "term": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 }, "definition": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 }, "aliases": { "type": "array", "items": { "type": "string" } }, "pattern": { "type": "string" }, "flags": { "type": "string", "default": "iu" }, "enabled": { "type": "boolean" }, "source": { "type": "string" } } }`.
